options: Molten NaCl contains free electrons, while solid NaCl does not.

questions.question: Which statement most accurately explains why ionic compounds have high melting points?
options: Ionic bonds are covalent in nature.
Large numbers of strong electrostatic attractions throughout the lattice require significant energy to overcome.
Individual ions possess high kinetic energy.
The ions are chemically bonded to every neighbouring atom.

questions.question: Which property would most strongly suggest that an unknown solid is metallic rather than ionic?
options: Electrical conductivity as a solid combined with malleability

questions.question: Which pair of substances would be expected to have the greatest difference in boiling point?
Which statement about intermolecular forces is the most accurate?
options: They determine many physical properties without changing molecular identity.
They are stronger than covalent bonds.

Bonding pairs always repel more strongly than lone pairs.

questions.question: Which compound would have the strongest intermolecular forces?
Which explanation best describes why branching lowers the boiling point of hydrocarbons?
options: Branched molecules have stronger covalent bonds.
Branched molecules have a smaller surface area, reducing intermolecular attractions.

questions.question: Which statement best distinguishes an alkene from an alkane?
options: Alkenes contain at least one carbon-carbon double bond, making them unsaturated.
Alkenes always have lower boiling points.

options: Methane has weaker intermolecular forces because it has fewer electrons and a smaller surface area.

questions.question: Which observation would provide the strongest evidence that combustion was incomplete?
options: Yellow smoky flame with soot deposits.

questions.question: Which statement about structural isomers is most correct?
options: They have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements.

options: They are non-polar while water is polar.

questions.question: Which is the best explanation for why longer hydrocarbons are less volatile?
options: Stronger intermolecular attractions require more energy for molecules to escape the liquid.
